page: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring an AMD EPYC processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core using shared CPU allocation. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 16 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a 300 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests, including both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block reads and writes, consistently register average tier results. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in text generation for small 135M parameter language models, and strong performance in text generation for medium 7B parameter models. Other operations, such as Redis SET transactions, static web serving throughput, and database operations, perform in the middle 50% tier.

The instance offers a compute-focused hardware profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high parallel processing capacity without massive memory footprints. The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads sensitive to noisy-neighbor performance fluctuations, though the strong single-core benchmark results mitigate some of these concerns. This server is suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, medium-scale database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores. Operating at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture, this server features a shared CPU allocation, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core processing and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance provides a balanced, compute-focused profile. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, database operations, and lightweight LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 300 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance and efficient text generation capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an AMD EPYC instance with 16 shared vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 300 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC compute instance featuring sixteen vCPUs, bundled NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for parallel workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory and 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
